method: Clone driven headlessly (--shot-eval probe): setDoc('oracle bold italic georgia'), bold on 'bold' via WC.Commands.run, italic on 'italic' via WC.Commands.run, Georgia + 20pt on 'georgia' via WC.Commands.comboCommit; exported via WC.PM.exportDocxBytes -> wordAPI.saveBytes to tests/fixtures/.oracle-out.docx (not checked in); read back through real Word via scripts/oracle/word-oracle.js read-props + read-word-props.
exportedXmlRuns: word/document.xml: run 'bold' has <w:b/>; run 'italic' has <w:i/>; run 'georgia' has <w:rFonts w:ascii='Georgia' .../> + <w:sz w:val='40'/> (40 half-points = 20pt); 'oracle ' and inter-word spaces unformatted.

paragraphLevelRead.granularityNote: Per-paragraph reads report MIXED formatting as bold=false / italic=false / fontName='' / fontSize=null (AppleScript missing value -> NaN -> null). A paragraph with one bold word therefore reports bold:false — this is the documented granularity limit, NOT a clone failure. Word-level read below is the real judgment.

wordLevelRead.generatedBy: word-oracle read-word-props (new verb, paragraph 1, all words)
wordLevelRead.note: Word's words collection includes each word's trailing space and the paragraph mark as a trailing empty 'word' (index 5). Range formatting evaluation ignores trailing whitespace — bold word + plain trailing space still reports bold=true.

no bleed onto neighbours.expected: words 1/3/4 not bold; words 1/2/4 not italic
no bleed onto neighbours.wordReported: confirmed — only word 2 bold, only word 3 italic

fontName Georgia on 'georgia'.verdict: PASS (word-level; paragraph-level reports '' because the paragraph mixes Aptos + Georgia)

fontSize 20pt on 'georgia'.verdict: PASS (raw AppleScript value was '20,0' — comma-locale decimal, quirk #13)

default font elsewhere.verdict: PASS — Word resolved the theme default for unformatted runs

overall: PASS — every attribute the clone applied came back from real Word exactly as applied, at run granularity. Paragraph-level 'mixed' returns are a documented oracle granularity limit, handled by the new read-word-props verb (committed separately).
pidSafety: Document count was 0 before and after every oracle invocation; only .oracle-out.docx was opened/closed (by name); Word never quit.
